skill.name: generating-solana-projects
skill.description: Generates Solana dApp project scaffolds with Anchor 0.32.1, Rust smart contract templates, TypeScript tests, and a Next.js wallet-connected frontend.
skill.summary: Create Anchor-based Solana dApp scaffolds with tests and a Next.js frontend.
skill.author: MSzgy

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer found many command, filesystem, network, and script indicators. Review shows these are mainly fenced templates, setup commands, local development URLs, and generated project paths, not confirmed malicious code. Because the skill can guide Bash and Write operations for blockchain projects, publish with a warning that generated commands and contracts require user review.

medium_findings.title: Command-Heavy Generated Project Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill includes Bash-capable setup guidance for installing dependencies, generating program IDs, building, deploying, testing, and starting a frontend. These commands are legitimate for Solana development, but users should inspect generated projects before running deployment or package-install commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced files clearly contain shell commands and the skill declares Bash and Write support. The context is normal developer scaffolding, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Default Wallet and Generated File Paths
low_findings.description: The templates mention a default Solana wallet path, relative imports to generated target files, and ignored build directories. These are expected Anchor and Next.js patterns and do not show secret reading or path traversal intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are inside documented generated project templates or standard local wallet configuration. No network transfer, file read operation, or credential extraction instruction was found.

low_findings.title: Local Development and Registry URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to an Anchor registry, localhost validator, and local frontend. They support normal development and do not indicate data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible configuration values used by Anchor and local Solana tooling. I found no hidden endpoint or instruction to send credentials externally.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Scanner Hits Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm locations appear in ordinary descriptive text and placeholders, not in cryptographic implementation code. No use of insecure hash or cipher APIs was found in the inspected context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are skill descriptions, feature bullets, or error-message placeholders. They do not call cryptographic functions or recommend weak algorithms.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash and Write Tooling Requires User Oversight
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill is permitted to use Bash and Write while creating project scaffolds. This is appropriate for code generation, but generated commands should not be run without inspection.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The manifest explicitly lists Bash and Write, and the workflow creates files for a full application. The declared purpose is legitimate, but the capability increases blast radius.

content.value_statement: Solana project setup requires many coordinated files across Anchor, Rust, TypeScript, and Next.js. This skill creates a complete scaffold with contracts, tests, wallet integration, and setup guidance.
actual_capabilities: Creates Anchor project structures with program, tests, and frontend folders.
Generates Rust smart contract templates with state, errors, and instruction handlers.
Adds TypeScript test scaffolds for Anchor-based Solana programs.
Builds a Next.js frontend outline with wallet adapter integration.
Provides setup steps for program IDs, build, deploy, test, and local frontend launch.
Uses fixed dependency versions documented in the skill reference.
limitations: Generated smart contracts still need security review before deployment.
It does not validate real tokenomics, governance rules, or financial assumptions.
Dependency versions may need updates for newer Solana or Next.js releases.
Deployment commands require local Solana, Anchor, Rust, and Node tooling.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Solana dApp
use_cases.description: Create a working project skeleton for voting, escrow, marketplace, staking, or custom Solana applications.

use_cases.title: Teach Anchor Project Structure
use_cases.description: Show how Anchor programs, tests, IDL files, and frontend clients fit together in one repository.

use_cases.title: Start Client-Facing Delivery
use_cases.description: Generate a consistent baseline before adding business rules, wallet flows, and production safeguards.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Project
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple Solana app scaffold.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Solana voting dApp with Anchor, TypeScript tests, and a Next.js frontend.

prompt_templates.title: Add Specific Instructions
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with contract behavior requirements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Solana escrow project with initialize, exchange, and cancel instructions plus tests for each path.

prompt_templates.title: Include Frontend Workflows
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request focused on wallet and user interface flows.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Solana NFT marketplace scaffold with listing, offers, wallet connection, and marketplace screens.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Complex Protocol
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that needs clear accounts, constraints, and review notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Solana staking protocol scaffold with reward accounting, admin controls, tests, frontend dashboard, and security review notes.

output: A project structure with Anchor program folders, voting account models, instruction handlers, and TypeScript tests.
A Next.js frontend outline with wallet connection, proposal views, and transaction actions.
Post-generation steps for program ID setup, build, deploy, tests, and frontend launch.

output_examples.input: Generate an NFT marketplace with listings and offers.
output: Contract scaffolding for listings, offers, acceptance, cancellation, and marketplace errors.
Frontend component plan for listing cards, creation forms, offer dialogs, and wallet state.
Testing guidance covering listing creation, offers, accepted offers, and cancellation cases.

output_examples.input: Build a token staking starter project.
output: Anchor account and instruction templates for pools, user stakes, unstaking, and reward claims.
A dashboard-oriented frontend structure for balances, rewards, and staking actions.
Clear setup instructions for local validator, deployment, tests, and development server.

best_practices: Review every generated smart contract before deploying to devnet or mainnet.
Run generated tests locally and add edge cases for authority, overflow, and account constraints.
Pin or update dependency versions intentionally before starting production work.
anti_patterns: Do not deploy generated contracts with placeholder program IDs.
Do not treat generated financial logic as audited production code.
Do not run package install or deploy commands before reviewing generated files.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy contracts automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides setup and deployment instructions, but users should run deployment commands themselves after review.

faq.question: Can it create both backend and frontend code?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to create Anchor program scaffolds, TypeScript tests, and a Next.js frontend outline.

faq.question: Is the generated smart contract code audited?
faq.answer: No. Generated contracts need manual security review and additional tests before any production deployment.

faq.question: Which tools are expected on my machine?
faq.answer: Generated projects expect Rust, Solana CLI, Anchor CLI, Node.js, and package manager tooling.

faq.question: Can it generate NFT, DAO, escrow, or staking projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples and templates cover these project types and can be adapted to custom Solana dApps.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatible workflows.
